ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: ACCOUNTING, REPORT
Off Calendar Nov-15-2017 Continued To Feb-07-18 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Trisha Friedeberg for and with Anthony Bryant.
Margaret Martin Court appointed Counsel for Alex Woodruff in the conservatorship matter.

Ms. Friedeberg stated that the budget was filed on October 16th, that Special Needs Trust money can not be used for food and shelter, and that she tried to file a one year accounting, but the Clerk rejected it. The Court stated that it should be a supplement. Ms. Friedeberg also stated that the Conservators are paying out of pocket when anything unexpected comes up, that the proposal they laid out shows how much money was spent and that the Trustee is to pay $10,000.00 back to the Trust for resolution of the accountings. The Court raised the issue of bond and a surcharge. Ms. Friedeberg expressed concern about legal fees associated with surcharge, stated that the Conservators are paying for legal fees on their own and that they prefer to pay the $10,000.00 on their own rather than by way of a surcharge. Ms. Martin raised the issue of the Court protecting Alex's interest going forward, spoke of Ms. Bryant's future care needs, the house being titled in Ms. Bryant's name not Alex's, stated that the focus should be on Alex's reimbursement being secured, that $10,000.00 is below what they started with and that this is a settlement offer. The Court raised the issue of mediation and placing a lien on Ms. Bryant's house. The Court signed the order referring the matter to Pro Bono Mediation. Mediation must be completed no later than January 17, 2018. The Parties are to notify the Probate Examiner as to the outcome of mediation by January 24, 2018. The First Account and Report of Trustee is continued to and the Parties are to return on February 7, 2018 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204 for status of mediation.

Judge: John K. Stewart. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: M. Nuque. Not reported.

ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: ACCOUNTING, REPORT
Off Calendar Oct-18-2017 Continued To Nov-15-17 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: ACCOUNTING, REPORT
Off Calendar Jul-26-2017 Continued To Aug-23-17 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Trisha Friedeberg for and with Mary Bryant (Trustee) and Anthony Bryant (Co-conservator).
Margaret Martin for and with Alex Woodruff (Beneficiary).

The Court stated that it received the latest declaration, that it is unfortunate that matter was not under Court supervision sooner, that it would be helpful to have a budget, and that it would like to see a more current one year accounting to see what the expenses are and what is needed. Ms. Friedeberg stated that she could have a budget within three weeks, that she will start working on the accounting and that Ms. Martin suggested meeting with a Pro Bono Mediator. Ms. Martin pointed out that this is a Special Needs Trust, that it would not be used for room and board, and that Mr. Woodruff requires extensive care. Ms. Martin also stated that the bank statements she requested were provided forthwith and that there has been full cooperation. The First Account and Report of Trustee, etc. is continued to August 23, 2017 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204.

Judge: John K. Stewart. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: M. Nuque. Not reported.

An Order to Appear will be issued to the Trustee Mary Bryant and the Attorney Trisha Friedeberg to appear on July 26, 2017 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204 to explain why they failed to appear. The First Account and Report of Trustee, etc. is also continued to that date.

Mr. Feder also stated that the accounting petition was filed on Friday with a hearing date of May 15, 2017. Status on filing of the first account is taken off calendar.
